content: For Rust LLM provider integration, async-openai (0.41.1, ~2M recent downloads, crates.io verified 2026-06-26) is the leading dedicated crate: supports streaming chat completions and tool/function calling against OpenAI's API, and works against any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (local Ollama/LM Studio, OpenRouter) by overriding base_url. rig-core (0.39.0, ~917K dl) is a higher-level opinionated framework (agent/RAG abstractions) — more than Scrysynth's thin provider impl needs. Scrysynth already has a clean synchronous PlannerProvider trait returning PlannerProviderOutput::Json/Typed, so the LLM provider is a new impl that calls a provider and returns JSON parsed into TypedCommand. reqwest 0.13.4 is the standard HTTP client if hand-rolling. Recommendation: async-openai via reqwest transport (or raw reqwest if avoiding the async runtime coupling), keeping the planner boundary synchronous and provider-agnostic.
